motivation: Instantiate thread routines via the Thread class.
whyExists: Network requests can take 200ms to 2000ms. Database queries take 10-500ms. Reading files takes 1-100ms. If programs blocked during these waits, a web server handling 1000 requests would be completely frozen most of the time, serving almost no requests. Asynchronous I/O allows servers to handle thousands of concurrent connections efficiently on limited resources.
realWorldAnalogy:
Imagine you're at a restaurant. You order food (start an async task). Instead of standing at the counter staring at the kitchen until your food is ready (blocking), you sit at your table and do other things — read the menu, chat with friends. When the food is ready, the waiter brings it to you (callback/promise resolves).

Async programming is exactly this: "Start this task, go do other things, come back to me when it's done."
simpleExplanation: To learn Runnable Thread from first principles, start by looking at how data resolves at runtime. Imagine your processor executing instructions sequentially. Runnable Thread defines how values bind, operations evaluate, and stack structures are clean.
visualDiagram:
```text
[User Input] ──► [Process Scope: Runnable Thread] ──► [Memory Address] ──► [Console Output]
```
syntaxExplanation: Verify that all brackets, colons, keywords, and assignments match the java syntax specifications. Avoid naming collisions by using scoped namespaces.

stepByStepExecution.desc: Allocate memory segment and load bindings.
